description: Connections are never closed, accumulating in CLOSE_WAIT until the pool and file descriptors are exhausted.
body: Failure mode: response bodies/connections are not fully read and closed, so connections sit in CLOSE_WAIT and are never returned to the pool; over time the pool is exhausted, new calls block, and the process hits its file-descriptor limit. Detection signals: rising CLOSE_WAIT socket count; pool acquisition blocking; 'too many open files' errors; latency growing as connections leak. Do not raise the FD limit to mask the leak. Safer fallback: close/return every connection and response body in finally/defer, size the pool with idle timeout and max lifetime, and verify socket counts stay flat under sustained load.
